name: baselight
description: Query thousands of public Baselight datasets (crypto, finance, climate, and more) with DuckDB SQL. Bundles the skill and the remote MCP server.

baselight_api_key.title: Baselight API key
baselight_api_key.description: Used to authenticate the MCP server. Leave blank to fall back to the BASELIGHT_API_KEY environment variable or ~/.baselight/credentials. Get a key at baselight.app → Account Settings → Integrations.

hooks.//: If a key was entered at install (userConfig), mirror it into ~/.baselight/credentials — replacing only the BASELIGHT_API_KEY line, preserving anything else — so the bundled skill (which reads that file) uses the same key as the MCP server. A blank prompt is a no-op, leaving hand-managed credentials untouched.
hooks.command: key='${user_config.baselight_api_key}'; [ -z "$key" ] && exit 0; d="$HOME/.baselight"; f="$d/credentials"; mkdir -p "$d"; t=$(grep -v '^BASELIGHT_API_KEY=' "$f" 2>/dev/null); { [ -n "$t" ] && printf '%s\n' "$t"; printf 'BASELIGHT_API_KEY=%s\n' "$key"; } > "$f"; chmod 600 "$f"

baselight.//: Resolves the x-api-key three ways: the key entered at install (userConfig), else $BASELIGHT_API_KEY, else ~/.baselight/credentials — matching the skill's flexibility. Plain $VAR (no braces) is left for the shell.
baselight.headersHelper: k="${user_config.baselight_api_key}"; [ -z "$k" ] && k="$BASELIGHT_API_KEY"; if [ -z "$k" ] && [ -f "$HOME/.baselight/credentials" ]; then k=$(grep -m1 '^BASELIGHT_API_KEY=' "$HOME/.baselight/credentials" | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '[:space:]'); fi; printf '{"x-api-key":"%s"}' "$k"
